texto: Did you hear about the lawsuit? Mary asked."No, what?""I hear that he is so big," she lifted her eyebrows to indicate what she meant, "that he put a girl in the hospital and she is suing him, because she can never have babies because of him.""Ewwwwwwwwwww!!!!" the sisters chorused."Could a guy really do that?" Lydia asked.Elizabeth shrugged. "I guess, but he would have to be the size of a friggin' oak tree.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Heather Lynn Rigaud que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Did you hear about the lawsuit? Mary asked."No, what?""I hear that he is so big," she lifted her …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, penis, trees, com foco em trees.

Mary asked."No, what?""I hear that he is so big," she lifted her eyebrows to indicate what she meant, "that he put a girl in the hospital and she is suing him, because she can never have babies because of him.""Ewwwwwwwwwww!!!!" the sisters chorused."Could a guy really do that?" texto: It seemed so good when it started.I gave my trust to you.I came to you open-hearted,Hoping it was true.Now I've gotten smart.Now I've learned some things.Now I know that what once was a start,Is just an ending.The longest good-byeI ever knew,The longest good-byeWas the dayI said hello to you.
explicacao: Citação de Heather Lynn Rigaud sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “It seemed so good when it started.I gave my trust to you.I came to you open-hearted,Hoping it was…” articula amor, goodbyes, hello-goodbye em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como termino e hello-goodbye.
